experimental task), you can define database tables using JSON.<div style="margin-top: 8px;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Each table is one JSON file.&nbsp;</span><div><div style="margin-top: 8px; margin-right: 0px;">You can POST data to your table via <font style="color: rgb(153, 0, 0);" face="Lucida Console">/table/&lt;name&gt;</font>, data gets automatically timestamped and associated with the participant.</div></div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="120" width="280" x="480" value="To add additional pages, add HTML files to one of these directories.&nbsp;<div style="margin-top: 8px;">"Instruction" pages display your HTML with BOFS chrome and display a "continue" button.</div><div style="margin-top: 8px;">"Simple" pages&nbsp;<span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">pages display your HTML with BOFS chrome, but without a "continue" button.</span></div><div style="margin-top: 8px;">"Custom" pages&nbsp;<span style="color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255)); background-color: transparent;">pages display your HTML without BOFS chrome, and without a "continue" button.</span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"></span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="165.66" width="280" x="480" y="474.34" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="xSClNSHaVlQnWdRqsnNF-35" parent="1" face="Lucida Console" style="color: rgb(153, 0, 0);">static/</font><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">&nbsp;directory.</span><div style="margin-top: 8px;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">These can be referenced from any template or questionnaire at the <font div.div:
Define settings, page flow, conditions, etc.
The file you reference in the BOFS command.
Filename can be anything you like.

div.div:
To store additional data (e.g., for an experimental task), you can define database tables using JSON.
Each table is one JSON file.
You can POST data to your table via /table/<name>, data gets automatically timestamped and associated with the participant.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 274px; height: 1px; padding-top: 407px; margin-left: 276px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;

div.div:
To add additional pages, add HTML files to one of these directories.
"Instruction" pages display your HTML with BOFS chrome and display a "continue" button.
"Simple" pages pages display your HTML with BOFS chrome, but without a "continue" button.
"Custom" pages pages display your HTML without BOFS chrome, and without a "continue" button.

div.div:
To add static assets (images, videos, audio clips, JavaScript), add them to the static/ directory.
These can be referenced from any template or questionnaire at the /static/<filename> URL.

div.div: Use "blueprints" to extend BOFS's functionality in any additional way. This exposes the underlying mechanisms for creating new routes in Python via the Flask web framework.
